investigation_summary: European regulators found misleading sustainability marketing (Sweden Conscious line; Norway Higg MSI ruling). Germany fined €35.3M for illegal employee surveillance. Living-wage pledges (2013/2018/2022) documented unmet. Fast fashion volume (~3B garments/year) structurally conflicts with green claims.

profile.tax_summary: Netherlands IP licensing structure. Global effective rate ~20–22% cited in analyst summaries. No major tax fraud cases in this record.
profile.legal_summary: 2022: Swedish Consumer Agency — misleading 'Conscious' claims; line pulled. 2022: Norwegian authority — misleading Higg MSI use. 2020: Hamburg DPA €35.3M — illegal surveillance of workforce personal data. US consumer class actions (gift cards, etc.).
profile.labor_summary: Good On You: Not Good Enough (2/5). Bangladesh/Cambodia/Myanmar factory wages documented as poverty wages. Living-wage commitments unmet per UK Policy Exchange synthesis. 2017: investigative report on incineration of unsold new garments in Denmark despite sustainability marketing.
profile.environmental_summary: Very high garment output; polyester microplastic load. 2022 regulatory greenwashing findings. Take-back: critics — most collected textiles downcycled/incinerated vs closed-loop recycling. CDP climate C (2023).
profile.political_summary: EU textile policy lobbying; reported opposition to stronger mandatory due diligence. Limited PAC analogue (Swedish HQ).
profile.product_health: Budget-line dyestuffs: EU testing has flagged azo-dye concerns in value segments (general literature; verify per market).
profile.executive_summary: Persson family control via dual-class shares; large Swedish philanthropic footprint reported; no corruption findings in this volume.
